title: Would you recommend taking Calculus III and Differential Equations together?
author: trvmlyncrl
body:
Happy summer guys!

I’m currently redoing my schedule for the fall. I am registered for calc III, but I’m stuck at 11 credits. I need at least 12 to be full time and keep my aid. I don’t really want to waste time doing classes that don’t pertain to my major (like liberal studies courses, I’ve taken a bunch already) but if I have to I will.

I have the option of taking differential equations next semester also. Do you all recommend doing them together?

My schedule:

Calc III

Physics I

(Diff Eq maybe)

an LBST course

ENGR 1202

comments.author: NamasteHands
comments.body: Diff eq with Safronov isn't too demanding, I see no reason not to take it on.

comments.author: TQLSoul
comments.body: Seconded. Safronov will teach you the material without asking too much. Show up, ask questions, it'll be fine. Your algebra skills will be more on display than your calc skills in Diff Eq, in my experience at least.
